review.note: Retraction notice, not the original article. All 4 claims are factual transcriptions of the editorial statement (T7); none is numerically recomputable, so no advisory is manufactured. Deterministic verdicts preserved exactly (none existed). 3 integrity signals routed as advisories; 1 irreducibly-private process fact abstained.

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed (T7/T1). c1 asserts the original article (DOI 10.3892/ol.2017.6728, Oncol Lett 14:4599-4604, 2017) is retracted. SKEPTIC: rests on this notice's own statement, but a retraction notice is a primary-source authority on its own retraction. CLAIMS-AUDITOR: no over-reach. Retraction status is a fixed-external-knowledge lookup (DESIGN §5 T1), so it is deterministically confirmable -- but NO numeric/lookup verifier ran here (n_findings=0). WOULD CONFIRM: a Crossref/Retraction-Watch lookup on the original DOI returning relation='is-retracted-by' -> retraction DOI 10.3892/ol.2024.14510 (ONCOLOGY LETTERS 28:377, 2024). Route to human until that verifier exists.
routed_to_human.quote:
the Editor has decided that this paper 
should be retracted from the Journal

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed (T7 image-duplication). c2: Transwell invasion data in Fig. 4B (p. 4602) are 'strikingly similar' to data in different form in other articles by different authors at different institutes. This is the core integrity signal that drove the retraction. DOMAIN-EXPERT (oncology): STK39 (a Ste20/SPAK kinase) modulating osteosarcoma invasion/proliferation is field-plausible biology -- plausibility is not the problem; data provenance is. METHODOLOGIST: a retraction notice has no method of its own to assess. SKEPTIC: the notice gives no panel coordinates or the specific cross-papers, so LITMUS cannot recompute a duplication verdict from this document. WOULD CONFIRM: run an image-duplication/forensic verifier on the original Fig. 4B against the named source figures (panel-level correlation / copy-move / PubPeer thread). Genuine concern, not code-settleable here -> human.
routed_to_human.quote:
certain of the 
Transwell invasion assay data shown in Fig. 4B on p. 4602 
were strikingly similar to data appearing in different form in 
other articles written by different authors at different research 
institutes

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed (T7 prior/duplicate publication). c3: the contentious data had already been published prior to submission to Oncology Letters. CLAIMS-AUDITOR: the notice is appropriately hedged ('contentious data'), no over-claim. SKEPTIC: the prior venue/date are not named in this notice, so the timeline cannot be reconstructed from this document alone. WOULD CONFIRM: a literature/timeline search establishing the earlier publication of the same Fig. 4B / Transwell data with submission-date ordering. A real duplicate-publication concern that code cannot settle from the notice -> human.
routed_to_human.quote:
the contentious data in the 
above article had already been published prior to its submission 
to

abstained.message: Genuinely unassessable. c4 ('the authors were asked for an explanation ... but the Editorial Office did not receive a reply') is a private editorial-process fact. No verifier across T0-T7 can observe editor<->author correspondence; it carries no number to recompute and no figure/data to screen. SKEPTIC/CLAIMS-AUDITOR/METHODOLOGIST/DOMAIN-EXPERT/INTEGRITY-SCREENER all bottom out at 'not checkable from available evidence'. Abstain > guess (DESIGN §3.4).

evidence.quote:
The authors were asked 
for an explanation to account for these concerns, but the 
Editorial Office did not receive a reply

details.reason: private editorial correspondence; no verifier observes it
